mission.title: linkerd: multicluster doesn't work on EKS
mission.description: multicluster doesn't work on EKS. Users encounter: "```msg="Error processing RemoteGatewayCreated: {gatewaySpec: GatewaySpec: {gatewayName: linkerd-gate".

steps.title: Identify linkerd feature symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your linkerd deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app=linkerd
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=linkerd -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for error: ````msg="Error processing RemoteGatewayCreated: {gatewaySpec: GatewaySpec: {gatewayName: linkerd-gateway, gatewayNamespac`

steps.title: Check current Service configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant linkerd resources:
```bash
kubectl get service -A
kubectl describe service <name> -n <namespace>
```
When attempting to link a source cluster to a target EKS cluster using the `linkerd multicluster link` command, the service mirror controller in the source cluster logs this error:

```msg="Error proc

steps.title: Apply the fix for multicluster doesn't work on EKS
steps.description:
### Issue: #4501 

After a few attempts at adding support for `EndpointSlice` resources, I came up with this poc to show one way of doing this.

### EndpointSlice access
---

* The first prerequisite to supporting `EndpointSlices` is to make sure that the cluster has access to the resource, since it
```yaml
and fails to create the endpoints object for the gateway mirror.

This happens because the service mirror controller attempts to copy the `hostname` and `IP` fields from the gateway service's ingress struct to the EndpointAddress struct of the gateway mirror's endpoints object.  In EKS, the ingress struct of the `linkerd-gateway` service is populated with a `hostname` and no `IP`.  However, the `IP` field of EndpointAddress is mandatory.
### Issue: #4501 

After a few attempts at adding support for `EndpointSlice` resources, I came up with this poc to show one way of doing this.

steps.title: Upgrade linkerd to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade linkerd:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade linkerd jetstack/linkerd --namespace cert-manager
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager
helm list -n cert-manager
```

steps.title: Confirm multicluster doesn't work on EKS is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=linkerd -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that ````msg="Error processing RemoteGatewayCreated: {gatewaySpec: GatewaySpec: {gatewayName: linkerd-gateway, gatewayNamespac` no longer appears in logs.

resolution.summary:
The root cause is: ### Issue: #4501 

After a few attempts at adding support for `EndpointSlice` resources, I came up with this poc to show one way of doing this.

### EndpointSlice access
---

* The first prerequisite to supporting `EndpointSlices` is to make sure that the cluster has access to the resource, since it sits behind a feature gate. To do this, we are using a similar check to `ServiceProfile`. First, we. This fixes the issue because it addresses the underlying problem that was causing the failure.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with linkerd installed or the issue environment reproducible.
